nodes.x: WHAT advantage then hath the Jew? or what profit [is there] of circumcision?

do the best we can; and what is it? There is a curious saying that Paul made on a certain occasion, in speaking about the Jews and the Gentiles: “What advantage hath the Jew? or what profit is there of circumcision? “Much every way: chiefly, that unto them were committed the oracles of God. “Whose are the fathers, and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came, who is over all, God blessed…
Much every way: chiefly, because that unto them were committed the oracles of God.
2 But he who is a Jew from the heart, I. say hath much every way; chiefly because-that unto them were committed the oracles of God.
For what if some did not believe? shall their unbelief make the faith of God without effect?
shall their unbelief make the faith of God without effect? see D&C 58:32
God forbid: yea, let God be true, but every man a liar; as it is written, That thou mightest be justified in thy sayings, and mightest overcome when thou art judged.
But if our unrighteousness commend the righteousness of God, what shall we say? [Is] God unrighteous who taketh vengeance? (I speak as a man )
God forbid: for then how shall God judge the world?
For if the truth of God hath more abounded through my lie unto his glory; why yet am I also judged as a sinner?
And not [rather], (as we be slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say,) Let us do evil, that good may come? whose damnation is just.
What then? are we better [than they]? No, in no wise: for we have before proved both Jews and Gentiles, that they are all under sin;
As it is written, There is none righteous, no, not one:
There is none that understandeth, there is none that seeketh after God.
14 °f He that seeketh’ God will receive discipline, And he that resorteth to Him diligently shall obtain *favours*.
They are all gone out of the way, they are together become unprofitable; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
6 And the anger of God kindleth against the inhabitants of the earth; and none doeth good, for all have gone out of the way.

 earth; and none doeth good, for all have gone out of the way.
Their throat [is] an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the poison of asps [is] under their lips:

lie, and worshiped and served the crea- ture more than the Creator, who is blessed forever. There is no fear of God before their eyes.
This corresponds to (Genesis 6:8), “grace in God’s eyes.” “God’s eyes” is the Supernal Colors, the concept of silver and gold. The primary grace and splendor is there.

CI. _Exhortation to the Fear of God: all Nature fears Him, but not the
                               Sinners._
Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.
Therefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight: for by the law [is] the knowledge of sin.
But now the righteousness of God without the law is manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets;
Even the righteousness of God [which is] by faith of Jesus Christ unto all and upon all them that believe: for there is no difference:
For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;
Romans 3:23 For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;

Repentance is a continuing process, needed by all because "all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God" (Romans 3:23). (Dallin H. Oaks, "Sins, Crimes, and Atonement", An address given to CES religious educators, 7 February 1992)
not obedient we shall come short of that glory that we anticipate now. I hope and pray that the Lord will bless you all. Amen.

unclean lips. Inasmuch as "all [save Jesus Christ] have sinned, and come short of the glory of God" (Rom. 3:23), Isaiah too has "unclean lips" and has to be cleansed (6:6-7). Likewise, Joseph Smith went through a purification process (D&C 29:3; JS 1:29). (Donald W. Parry, Jay A. Parry, and Tina M. Peterson, Understanding Isaiah, p.66)
Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:
Whom God hath set forth [to be] a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;

Propitiation. Jesus Christ is the "propitiation for our sins" (1 Jn. 2:2; 4:10). It is he "whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God" (Rom. 3:25). It is he who propitiates or appeases justice for the purpose of reuniting God and man. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and…

And so, according to the law of propitiation, Christ came to appease the demands of divine justice and effect a reconciliation between God and man. Thus John taught: "Jesus Christ the righteous is the propitiation for our sins: and not for ours only, but also [on conditions of repentance] for the sins of the whole world." (1 John 2:1-2.) Our theological friend Paul expounds this doctrine by…

prove that because death has followed all men, the atonement of Christ was incompetent, and did not accomplish the end for which it was intended, he sums up by stating that the mission of Messiah was, merely to be a Savior of men by becoming a preacher of righteousness, and that He fell a martyr to the truth only, as did the prophets who were slain previous to His coming. With all his learning…
To declare, [I say], at this time his righteousness: that he might be just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.
Where [is] boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? of works? Nay: but by the law of faith.
Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.
[Is he] the God of the Jews only? [is he] not also of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also:
Both Jews and Gentiles asked for the repetition of the sermon
Seeing [it is] one God, which shall justify the circumcision by faith, and uncircumcision through faith.
Do we then make void the law through faith? God forbid: yea, we establish the law.
